# UX Data Performance API Security Checklist: Configure Alert Thresholds and Notification Channels

**Purpose**: Validate the quality of requirements across UX, data, performance, API, and security concerns for UC-13 before implementation work begins.
**Created**: 2026-03-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](/Users/sahmed/Documents/311-forecast-system/specs/013-uc-13-configure-alert-thresholds/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the operator-facing configuration needs defined beyond a simple save action, including what must be visible for the active configuration, supported channel availability, scoped threshold rules, and scoped delivery preferences? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK002 Are the required attributes of the shared active configuration fully specified, including immutable versioning, active-marker behavior, threshold rules, channel selections, and per-scope frequency or deduplication controls? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Data Model Direction, Data Model §AlertConfigurationVersion]
- [x] CHK003 Are requirements defined for all save outcomes that users and operators must handle, including validation rejection, successful activation, and storage failure while preserving the previous active configuration? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-014 through §FR-020, Data Model §AlertConfigurationUpdateAttempt]
- [x] CHK004 Are all API consumers and behaviors covered for both reading and replacing the active configuration, not just the UI persona editing settings? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §paths]
- [x] CHK005 Does the specification define what performance expectations apply to both active-configuration loading and synchronous save handling, rather than only stating that the workflow is interactive? [Gap, Spec §Success Criteria, Plan §Performance Goals]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is “future alerts match my operational workflow” translated into observable configuration behavior for threshold selection, channel choice, and scoped frequency or deduplication effects rather than left as outcome-only wording? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-021, Spec §FR-024]
- [x] CHK007 Is “supported notification channel” unambiguous about the difference between a channel type the platform can expose and a channel currently available at load or save time? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-011 through §FR-013, Plan §Implementation Notes, Contract §SupportedChannel]
- [x] CHK008 Is “frequency or deduplication preferences” precise enough to determine which combinations are allowed, which fields are required together, and whether a scope may omit both controls entirely? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-014, Data Model §AlertConfigurationDeliveryPreference, Contract §DeliveryPreferenceInput]
- [x] CHK009 Is the concept of “one shared active configuration” clear enough to distinguish it from drafts, superseded versions, and save-attempt records during UI reloads and downstream alert evaluation? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §ActiveAlertConfigurationMarker, Data Model §AlertConfigurationUpdateAttempt]
- [x] CHK010 Is “optional geography scope” defined clearly enough to avoid conflicting interpretations of how category-only and category-plus-geography rules are created, displayed, validated, and matched later? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-022, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §Scope Type]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are the active-configuration continuity requirements consistent between the user stories, functional requirements, implementation notes, and update-attempt invariants? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-020, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §AlertConfigurationUpdateAttempt]
- [x] CHK012 Are validation rules for thresholds, channels, and delivery preferences consistent between the spec language and the contract or data-model constraints that define required fields and invalid combinations? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009 through §FR-014, Data Model §Validation rules, Contract §ThresholdRuleInput, Contract §DeliveryPreferenceInput]
- [x] CHK013 Are the assumptions about upstream supported-channel provisioning consistent with the plan’s decision to validate channel availability at both load and save time? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK014 Are the save-outcome vocabularies used consistently across spec, plan, data model, and API contract so that frontend and backend behavior share one canonical meaning? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §API Contract Direction, Data Model §Save Outcome, Contract §SaveOutcome]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can the success criteria objectively verify user-facing configuration value, or do they mainly confirm persistence and logging without proving the editor supports safe review and correction of scoped settings? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001 through §SC-005]
- [x] CHK016 Are performance targets measurable for the critical paths of active-configuration retrieval and synchronous save completion, rather than only described qualitatively in the plan? [Measurability, Spec §Success Criteria, Plan §Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK017 Can the requirement that successful and failed updates remain traceable be objectively verified without a more explicit definition of required audit fields, correlation identifiers, and operator-visible save identifiers? [Measurability, Spec §FR-017 through §FR-019, Spec §SC-002, Spec §SC-004, Data Model §AlertConfigurationUpdateAttempt]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are primary UX scenarios defined for loading the active configuration, editing scoped threshold and delivery rules, saving valid changes, and recovering after validation or storage failure? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK019 Are alternate data scenarios specified for configurations that change only channels, only delivery preferences, or only one scope while leaving unrelated scopes untouched? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-023, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK020 Are recovery requirements defined for what the UI should show after a validation rejection or storage failure, including whether the draft remains editable, whether the active snapshot is reloaded, and how the prior active version is identified? [Coverage, Recovery Flow, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Contract §AlertConfigurationValidationErrorResponse, Contract §AlertConfigurationStorageFailureResponse]
- [x] CHK021 Are API error and authorization scenarios covered by written requirements for unauthenticated access, unauthorized role use, malformed payloads, and stale or unsupported channel selections? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-001, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §paths]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK022 Are boundary-case requirements defined for duplicate threshold or delivery-preference scopes within the same save request, especially when category-only and category-plus-geography records coexist for the same category? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-022 through §FR-024, Data Model §AlertConfigurationThresholdRule, Data Model §AlertConfigurationDeliveryPreference]
- [x] CHK023 Are concurrency requirements defined for overlapping save requests from different authorized managers trying to replace the single active configuration at nearly the same time? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-005, Plan §Performance Goals, Data Model §ActiveAlertConfigurationMarker]
- [x] CHK024 Are edge cases specified for supported channels that are listed on initial page load but become unavailable before save validation completes? [Edge Case, Assumption, Spec §FR-011 through §FR-013, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK025 Are API payload-size, rule-count, and result-size boundaries defined for large shared configurations with many scoped threshold and delivery-preference entries? [Edge Case, Gap, Plan §Scale/Scope, Contract §ActiveAlertConfigurationResponse, Contract §AlertConfigurationSaveRequest]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK026 Are UX requirements defined for how field-level validation errors, save-success confirmations, storage-failure messages, and supported-channel availability states should be presented to operational managers? [Non-Functional, UX, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-016, Spec §FR-019]
- [x] CHK027 Are data retention and auditability requirements specified for superseded configuration versions, active-marker history, threshold rules, delivery preferences, channel selections, and save-attempt records? [Non-Functional, Data, Gap, Spec §FR-017 through §FR-020, Plan §Summary, Data Model §Relationships]
- [x] CHK028 Are performance requirements quantified for both interactive load latency and synchronous save completion under realistic shared-configuration sizes? [Non-Functional, Performance, Gap, Plan §Performance Goals, Plan §Scale/Scope]
- [x] CHK029 Are security requirements specific about role boundaries for reading versus updating configuration, token-based authentication, and protection against unauthorized alteration of the shared active configuration? [Non-Functional, Security, Plan §Constitution Check, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §securitySchemes]
- [x] CHK030 Are reliability and operational-safety requirements defined for atomic activation, partial-write prevention, and continued downstream alert evaluation when a save attempt fails after validation? [Non-Functional, Reliability, Spec §FR-020, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §Derived Invariants]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Are upstream dependencies on the supported-channel capability layer, platform authentication, and downstream alerting workflows explicitly documented as requirement constraints rather than implicit implementation assumptions? [Dependencies, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Technical Context, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK032 Is the assumption that threshold-range and delivery-preference validation policies already exist validated strongly enough, or should the spec define the minimum contract those policies must expose to UC-13? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-009 through §FR-014]
- [x] CHK033 Are dependency requirements defined for downstream consumers that read the active configuration marker, including when new versions become visible and what data they may rely on from the active snapshot? [Dependency, Gap, Spec §FR-021, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §Derived Invariants]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK034 Is there any conflict between the spec’s “one shared active configuration” wording and the plan’s immutable-version approach where successful saves create new snapshots rather than mutating a single record in place? [Conflict, Spec §FR-005, Plan §Summary, Data Model §AlertConfigurationVersion]
- [x] CHK035 Is it established clearly enough whether delivery preferences are optional per scope, given the spec says frequency controls, deduplication controls, or both may be configured, while the data model requires at least one of those control types when a preference record exists? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-008, Data Model §AlertConfigurationDeliveryPreference, Contract §DeliveryPreferenceInput]
- [x] CHK036 Are the terms “active configuration,” “configuration version,” “draft configuration,” “supported channel,” and “save outcome” used consistently enough across spec, plan, data model, and API contract to support one canonical UX and API vocabulary? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Service Direction, Data Model §Canonical UC-13 Vocabulary, Contract §components.schemas]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline.
- This checklist is intentionally lightweight and author-oriented, with equal coverage across UX, data, performance, API, and security requirement quality.
